reframing: Original spike goal was 'fine-tune timing on T4'. After Spike E identified ZipVoice-Distill as the base model supporting zero-shot cloning, we reframed to answer the higher-value question first: is zero-shot good enough to ship, deleting Phase 2 entirely?

environment.pipeline: piper_phonemize -> ONNX text_encoder -> ONNX fm_decoder (8 NFE, guidance=3.0) -> Vocos vocoder -> WAV

voices_tested.name: Alex
voices_tested.prompt_transcript: Hello, I can provide conversational narration in English with an authentic European French accent.
voices_tested.dan_verdict: Almost-GREEN — tone correct, sounds like worse microphone (reverb, flow issues)

voices_tested.name: Marcel
voices_tested.prompt_transcript: Hi, this is Marcel. What can I do for you today? For sure, I'll take care of it right away.
voices_tested.dan_verdict: Between Alex and Felix — identity recognizable, quality degraded

voices_tested.name: Felix
voices_tested.prompt_transcript: I noticed the old tree had something strange carved into its bark. Symbols, lines that didn't look like they belonged there.
voices_tested.dan_verdict: GREEN and near perfect — RP accent carries cleanly, no audible artifacts

timings.notes: Wall-clock on Lightning CPU through full Python pipeline. Irrelevant to ship architecture — browser runs inference on user's GPU/CPU, not Lightning. Spike B's WASM numbers are what matter for deployment.

conditions: Prompt clip length >= 10 seconds recommended (Felix at 8.3s was 'near perfect'; Alex at 6.5s showed artifacts)
Clean speech required (no music, no reverb, single speaker)
English training-distribution voices tested; non-English + heavy regional accents may still need fine-tuning

implications.phase_1_scope_reduction: No training_pipeline/ package needed. backend/training.py Deep Clone scaffold can be parked. Focus on Clone tab UX (enforce 10s+ prompt, auto-transcribe with Whisper) and Reader client rewrite (Phase 3).
implications.voice_studio_ux: Upload 10-15s clip -> auto-transcribe -> publish. No training step, no waiting hours. Per-voice quality comparable to commercial TTS (ElevenLabs, Speechify) based on subjective listen.
